text.title: decimal-as-string-pattern Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for decimal-as-string-pattern as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Modeling currency as a JSON number (float) and "rounding to two decimals at the end".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Compounding drift across many line items pushes totals off by one or more cents, which fails reconciliation. The Base-10 digit-encoding paper shows LLMs emit numbers as digit tokens anyway, so a string field is closer to the model's native representation while gaining exact arithmetic on the consumer side.

failure-mode.description: Reusing one wide pattern (^\S+$) for every string field "just in case".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Any string passes — the pattern adds zero decode-time constraint and earns false confidence. If the format is genuinely unknown, omit the pattern; otherwise pin the exact shape.
